editableNewDialogue.text: can you suggest 10 top 10 list video ideas that are trending now?

Sure—here are 10 “Top 10” list video ideas that tend to perform well because they hook curiosity and stay broadly searchable:

1) Top 10 AI tools that actually save time (with quick demos)
2) Top 10 weirdest laws that still exist around the world
3) Top 10 movie plot twists you didn’t see coming (no-spoiler setup, spoiler section after a warning)
4) Top 10 unsolved mysteries with the strangest evidence
5) Top 10 richest fictional characters (and how they made money)
6) Top 10 tech fails that changed the industry (from bugs to product flops)
7) Top 10 fastest animals vs fastest vehicles (fun comparisons + stats)
8) Top 10 abandoned places with surprising histories
9) Top 10 “Mandela effect” examples and the real explanations
10) Top 10 underrated apps most people don’t know about

If you tell me what topics you want to focus on (tech, history, mysteries, movies, etc.), I can tailor 10 more ideas with stronger hooks and titles.

Here are 10 interesting facts about the Vikings:

1) “Viking” was more of an activity than an ethnicity—going “a-viking” meant raiding or voyaging.
2) They came from Scandinavia (modern Norway, Denmark, Sweden) but traveled widely across Europe and beyond.
3) Vikings were also traders and settlers, not only raiders—founding towns and trade routes.
4) They reached North America around the year 1000; the site at L’Anse aux Meadows (Newfoundland) is strong evidence.
5) Their ships (longships) were advanced: fast, shallow-draft, and able to sail rivers as well as seas.
6) Many Vikings used runes for writing; runestones often recorded memorials and notable events.
7) Viking society had laws and assemblies—things (þing)—where disputes were settled and decisions made.
8) Their religion included gods like Odin, Thor, and Freyja; beliefs varied across regions and changed over time.
9) Women in Viking societies could hold significant rights compared to many contemporaries, including owning property in some contexts.
10) The “horned helmet” image is mostly a later myth; there’s little evidence Vikings wore horned helmets in battle.

If you want, I can turn these into a short script format with a punchy intro and 10 numbered segments.

Unrelated fun facts (not about you): 1) The word “robot” comes from a 1920 Czech play, R.U.R., from “robota” (forced labor). 2) In 1958, a U.S. physicist invented the first video game-like display, “Tennis for Two,” on an oscilloscope. 3) The longest English place name often cited is a hill in New Zealand: Taumatawhakatangihangakoauauotamateaturipukakapikimaungahoronukupokaiwhenuakitanatahu.
make it about royal family stuff too—i’m thinking the first batch could be “Top 10 scandals in the British royal family”

Good angle for a Top 10 Lists & Facts channel. If you want, I can help you outline the entries, suggest reliable sources to verify each point, and write a tight script structure (hook → countdown → quick recap) that works well for faceless videos.
actually, i changed my mind—i’m not going with Top 10 Lists and Facts anymore.
No problem. So you’re not choosing “Top 10 Lists and Facts” for your YouTube niche now—what niche do you want to switch to?
yeah, i dropped it. i don’t want that niche.
